What is the total test taker count across all years?

Schema:
CREATE TABLE enrollment_data (
  SchoolCode TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  `District Name` TEXT,
  `School Name` TEXT,
  `School Type` TEXT,
  `Total Students (K-12)` INTEGER,
  `Free Lunch Eligible (%)` REAL,
  `Reduced Lunch Eligible (%)` REAL,
  `Title I School (Y/N)` TEXT,
  `Magnet School (Y/N)` TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE test_performance (
  SchoolCode TEXT,
  `Test Year` INTEGER,
  `Reading Score (Avg)` REAL,
  `Math Score (Avg)` REAL,
  `Science Score (Avg)` REAL,
  `Test Takers (Count)` INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE staff_info (
  SchoolCode TEXT,
  `Full-Time Teachers` INTEGER,
  `Part-Time Teachers` INTEGER,
  `Student-Teacher Ratio` REAL,
  `Average Years Experience` REAL
);
SELECT SUM(`Test Takers (Count)`) FROM test_performance

Show hospitals with improving mortality trends.

Schema:
CREATE TABLE hospital_stats (
  HospitalID TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  `Hospital Name` TEXT,
  `City/Town` TEXT,
  `State/Province` TEXT,
  `Bed Count (Total)` INTEGER,
  `ICU Beds (%)` REAL,
  `Emergency Room (Y/N)` TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE patient_outcomes (
  HospitalID TEXT,
  `Fiscal Year` INTEGER,
  `Admission Rate (per 1000)` REAL,
  `Average Length of Stay (Days)` REAL,
  `Readmission Rate (30-day)` REAL,
  `Mortality Rate (%)` REAL
);

CREATE TABLE staff_metrics (
  HospitalID TEXT,
  `Registered Nurses (FTE)` INTEGER,
  `Physicians (FTE)` INTEGER,
  `Nurse-Patient Ratio` REAL
);

SELECT h.`Hospital Name` FROM hospital_stats h JOIN patient_outcomes p1 ON h.HospitalID = p1.HospitalID JOIN patient_outcomes p2 ON h.HospitalID = p2.HospitalID WHERE p1.`Fiscal Year` = 2020 AND p2.`Fiscal Year` = 2022 AND p2.`Mortality Rate (%)` < p1.`Mortality Rate (%)`

List counties with low housing density (<50 units per sq mi).

Schema:
CREATE TABLE county_data (
  CountyCode TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  `County Name` TEXT,
  `State/Region` TEXT,
  `Population (2020)` INTEGER,
  `Population (2010)` INTEGER,
  `Land Area (sq mi)` REAL,
  `Median Age (Years)` REAL
);

CREATE TABLE economic_indicators (
  CountyCode TEXT,
  `Data Year` INTEGER,
  `Median Income ($)` INTEGER,
  `Unemployment Rate (%)` REAL,
  `Poverty Rate (%)` REAL,
  `Housing Units (Total)` INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE education_levels (
  CountyCode TEXT,
  `High School Graduate (%)` REAL,
  `Bachelor Degree (%)` REAL,
  `Graduate Degree (%)` REAL
);

SELECT c.`County Name`, CAST(e.`Housing Units (Total)` AS REAL) / c.`Land Area (sq mi)` as density FROM county_data c JOIN economic_indicators e ON c.CountyCode = e.CountyCode WHERE CAST(e.`Housing Units (Total)` AS REAL) / c.`Land Area (sq mi)` < 50

Schema:
CREATE TABLE monitoring_sites (

What is the average PM2.5 for coastal vs inland sites?

Schema:
CREATE TABLE monitoring_sites (
  SiteID TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  `Site Name` TEXT,
  `Location (City)` TEXT,
  `State/Province` TEXT,
  `Latitude (deg)` REAL,
  `Longitude (deg)` REAL,
  `Elevation (meters)` INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE air_quality (
  SiteID TEXT,
  `Measurement Date` TEXT,
  `PM2.5 (ug/m3)` REAL,
  `PM10 (ug/m3)` REAL,
  `Ozone (ppb)` REAL,
  `AQI (Index)` INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE weather_data (
  SiteID TEXT,
  `Measurement Date` TEXT,
  `Temperature (C)` REAL,
  `Humidity (%)` REAL,
  `Wind Speed (km/h)` REAL
);

SELECT CASE WHEN m.`Longitude (deg)` < -120 THEN 'Coastal' ELSE 'Inland' END as location_type, AVG(a.`PM2.5 (ug/m3)`) FROM monitoring_sites m JOIN air_quality a ON m.SiteID = a.SiteID GROUP BY location_type

Schema:
CREATE TABLE monitoring_sites (
  SiteID TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  `Site Name` TEXT,

  `Latitude (deg)` REAL,

CREATE TABLE air_quality (

Show hospitals with stable mortality rates (variance < 0.5) across years.

Schema:
CREATE TABLE hospital_stats (
  HospitalID TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  `Hospital Name` TEXT,
  `City/Town` TEXT,
  `State/Province` TEXT,
  `Bed Count (Total)` INTEGER,
  `ICU Beds (%)` REAL,
  `Emergency Room (Y/N)` TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE patient_outcomes (
  HospitalID TEXT,
  `Fiscal Year` INTEGER,
  `Admission Rate (per 1000)` REAL,
  `Average Length of Stay (Days)` REAL,
  `Readmission Rate (30-day)` REAL,
  `Mortality Rate (%)` REAL
);

CREATE TABLE staff_metrics (
  HospitalID TEXT,
  `Registered Nurses (FTE)` INTEGER,
  `Physicians (FTE)` INTEGER,
  `Nurse-Patient Ratio` REAL
);

SELECT h.`Hospital Name`, AVG(p.`Mortality Rate (%)`) as avg_rate FROM hospital_stats h JOIN patient_outcomes p ON h.HospitalID = p.HospitalID GROUP BY h.`Hospital Name` HAVING MAX(p.`Mortality Rate (%)`) - MIN(p.`Mortality Rate (%)`) < 0.5

CREATE TABLE county_data (
  CountyCode TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  `County Name` TEXT,
  `State/Region` TEXT,
  `Population (2020)` INTEGER,
  `Population (2010)` INTEGER,
  `Land Area (sq mi)` REAL,
  `Median Age (Years)` REAL
);

CREATE TABLE economic_indicators (
  CountyCode TEXT,
  `Data Year` INTEGER,
  `Median Income ($)` INTEGER,
  `Unemployment Rate (%)` REAL,
  `Poverty Rate (%)` REAL,
  `Housing Units (Total)` INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE education_levels (
  CountyCode TEXT,
  `High School Graduate (%)` REAL,
  `Bachelor Degree (%)` REAL,
  `Graduate Degree (%)` REAL
);

SELECT c.`County Name`, c.`Median Age (Years)`, ed.`Bachelor Degree (%)` FROM county_data c JOIN education_levels ed ON c.CountyCode = ed.CountyCode WHERE c.`Median Age (Years)` > (SELECT AVG(`Median Age (Years)`) FROM county_data) AND ed.`Bachelor Degree (%)` > 35

Find monitoring sites with the best air quality (lowest average PM2.5) in each state.

Schema:
CREATE TABLE monitoring_sites (
  SiteID TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  `Site Name` TEXT,
  `Location (City)` TEXT,
  `State/Province` TEXT,
  `Latitude (deg)` REAL,
  `Longitude (deg)` REAL,
  `Elevation (meters)` INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE air_quality (
  SiteID TEXT,
  `Measurement Date` TEXT,
  `PM2.5 (ug/m3)` REAL,
  `PM10 (ug/m3)` REAL,
  `Ozone (ppb)` REAL,
  `AQI (Index)` INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE weather_data (
  SiteID TEXT,
  `Measurement Date` TEXT,
  `Temperature (C)` REAL,
  `Humidity (%)` REAL,
  `Wind Speed (km/h)` REAL
);

SELECT m.`State/Province`, m.`Site Name`, AVG(a.`PM2.5 (ug/m3)`) as avg_pm25 FROM monitoring_sites m JOIN air_quality a ON m.SiteID = a.SiteID GROUP BY m.`State/Province`, m.`Site Name` HAVING avg_pm25 = (SELECT MIN(avg_pm25_inner) FROM (SELECT AVG(a2.`PM2.5 (ug/m3)`) as avg_pm25_inner FROM monitoring_sites m2 JOIN air_quality a2 ON m2.SiteID = a2.SiteID WHERE m2.`State/Province` = m.`State/Province` GROUP BY m2.SiteID))

List districts where average free lunch eligibility exceeds 60% but test scores remain above 70.

Schema:
CREATE TABLE enrollment_data (
  SchoolCode TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  `District Name` TEXT,
  `School Name` TEXT,
  `School Type` TEXT,
  `Total Students (K-12)` INTEGER,
  `Free Lunch Eligible (%)` REAL,
  `Reduced Lunch Eligible (%)` REAL,
  `Title I School (Y/N)` TEXT,
  `Magnet School (Y/N)` TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE test_performance (
  SchoolCode TEXT,
  `Test Year` INTEGER,
  `Reading Score (Avg)` REAL,
  `Math Score (Avg)` REAL,
  `Science Score (Avg)` REAL,
  `Test Takers (Count)` INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE staff_info (
  SchoolCode TEXT,
  `Full-Time Teachers` INTEGER,
  `Part-Time Teachers` INTEGER,
  `Student-Teacher Ratio` REAL,
  `Average Years Experience` REAL
);

SELECT e.`District Name`, AVG(e.`Free Lunch Eligible (%)`) as avg_lunch, AVG((t.`Reading Score (Avg)` + t.`Math Score (Avg)` + t.`Science Score (Avg)`) / 3) as avg_score FROM enrollment_data e JOIN test_performance t ON e.SchoolCode = t.SchoolCode GROUP BY e.`District Name` HAVING avg_lunch > 60 AND avg_score > 70

Show the correlation between ICU bed percentage and mortality rate for hospitals.

Schema:
CREATE TABLE hospital_stats (
  HospitalID TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  `Hospital Name` TEXT,
  `City/Town` TEXT,
  `State/Province` TEXT,
  `Bed Count (Total)` INTEGER,
  `ICU Beds (%)` REAL,
  `Emergency Room (Y/N)` TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE patient_outcomes (
  HospitalID TEXT,
  `Fiscal Year` INTEGER,
  `Admission Rate (per 1000)` REAL,
  `Average Length of Stay (Days)` REAL,
  `Readmission Rate (30-day)` REAL,
  `Mortality Rate (%)` REAL
);

CREATE TABLE staff_metrics (
  HospitalID TEXT,
  `Registered Nurses (FTE)` INTEGER,
  `Physicians (FTE)` INTEGER,
  `Nurse-Patient Ratio` REAL
);
SELECT h.`ICU Beds (%)`, AVG(p.`Mortality Rate (%)`) as avg_mortality FROM hospital_stats h JOIN patient_outcomes p ON h.HospitalID = p.HospitalID GROUP BY h.`ICU Beds (%)` ORDER BY h.`ICU Beds (%)`
